assertions.text: V1 was traveling east on the exit ramp (implies motion eastward prior to departures).
V2 (tractor-trailer) was parked on the paved shoulder of the exit ramp facing east (stationary, oriented east).
V1 departed the roadway to the left, then returned to the roadway, then departed the roadway to the right before making contact with V2.
V1's front plane contacted the back plane of V2's trailer (V1 struck V2 from behind, impacting V2's rear).
There was a single impact event where V1's front contacted V2's trailer back plane.
V2 was pushed forward by the impact and came to rest near the point of impact.
V1 bounced back after impact and came to rest on the left shoulder facing east.
The rotation and bounce back of V1 occurred after the contact with V2.
The collision generated enough force to push V2 forward (non-negligible impact).

verdicts.evidence: V1 was traveling east prior to departures supported by pre-crash speed around [98.0 km/h](T2) at t=[-4.75 s](T1) and [96.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T1), indicating sustained forward motion.

Whether V2 was parked and oriented east cannot be determined from V1 telemetry; no telemetry channels report another vehicle's position or orientation, and there is no GPS heading in the provided channels so the claim is unverifiable from the sensors.

Lateral departures and the sequence left-return-right cannot be resolved with available channels: steering shows a peak of [40.5 deg](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T2) but without continuous lateral position or GPS heading the described sequence is unverifiable.

Impact direction (front plane contacting rear plane of another vehicle) requires relative-vehicle-frame impact direction; the crash-pulse shows longitudinal delta-V negative [final ~-98.2 km/h](T2) which indicates strong deceleration consistent with a frontal impact but axis alignment to vehicle front vs rear is not documented, so the specific front-to-rear contact is unverifiable.

Telemetry shows a single dominant crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [−98.6 km/h](T3) at t=[0.24 s](T3) and detect_events reported one impact_delta_v, consistent with a single impact event.
Whether V2 was pushed forward and came to rest near impact is about V2 motion; V1 sensors do not record V2 position or motion, so this is unverifiable from V1 telemetry.

Post-impact rotation of V1 is not directly given: steering peak is [40.5 deg](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T2) but there are no gyro or yaw-rate integrated rotation values available to confirm clockwise rotation, so the claim is unverifiable.

Bounce-back and final rest location on left shoulder facing east involve vehicle displacement and heading after impact; available telemetry lacks GPS position/heading after impact, so this is unverifiable.

The rotation/bounce sequence is stated to occur after contact; telemetry identifies the crash pulse at t≈[0.24 s](T3) and steering peak at t=[0.0 s](T2); while steering peaked at impact, detect_events shows the crash pulse occurrence and there is no earlier second impact, supporting that post-impact motions occurred after the recorded impact event.

The collision produced a large cumulative longitudinal delta-V with max magnitude [98.6 km/h](T3) (reported as [−98.6 km/h](T2/T3) signed), which is strong evidence of a high-force impact capable of moving another vehicle.

result.rationale: V1 telemetry strongly shows a single, very large longitudinal crash pulse ([−98.6 km/h](T3)) and sustained pre-crash forward speed ([98.0 → 96.0 km/h](T2)), supporting that V1 was traveling and underwent a major frontal deceleration. However, many assertions about V2 motion, relative orientation, lateral departures, and post-impact pose are about the other vehicle or require position/heading data not present in V1 sensors and are therefore unverifiable.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: ve longitudinal delta-V with max magnitude   (reported as [−98.6 km/h](T2/T3) signed), which is strong evidence of a high-force i

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
